summary: Current ATLAS homepage preserves the upstream template’s fixed-width body, table-based navigation, and simple single-file delivery, but it materially diverges in three places: the banner system was replaced with a custom gradient panel and one-line title treatment, the homepage information architecture was converted from the template’s page-route navigation into anchored in-page sections, and several custom content presentation styles were added (subtitle block, quote/question callouts, bulleted scope list). The minimum Builder change set to restore template alignment while preserving ATLAS content is to reintroduce the template banner/title composition and navigation proportions, while keeping the single-page sections and existing workshop copy.
work_completed: Compared /Users/yashturkar/Workspace/atlas-corl-2026/index.html against /tmp/conference-website-template/index.html for structure, banner, navigation, and information architecture.

Compared /Users/yashturkar/Workspace/atlas-corl-2026/assets/main.css against /tmp/conference-website-template/assets/main.css for layout system, banner treatment, navigation styling, and custom content presentation.
Checked docs/product-specs/workshop-website.md and docs/design-docs/site-architecture.md to distinguish intentional product-level deviations from accidental template drift.

findings.message: Banner treatment is the largest template break. Upstream uses an actual banner image plus overlaid split-color wordmark (``, `.title1`, `.title2`, `.year`) at /tmp/conference-website-template/index.html:17-25 with matching styles at /tmp/conference-website-template/assets/main.css:79-87. Current ATLAS replaces that with a synthetic `.banner-panel` gradient block, removes the image entirely, and collapses the title into a single stacked `ATLAS` plus subtitle at /Users/yashturkar/Workspace/atlas-corl-2026/index.html:20-30 and /Users/yashturkar/Workspace/atlas-corl-2026/assets/main.css:95-139. If Builder only fixes one area, fix this first.

Navigation pattern diverges structurally from the upstream information architecture. Upstream homepage nav is a five-cell route table linking to separate pages (`.`, `registration`, `program`, `directions`, `flyer`) at /tmp/conference-website-template/index.html:27-45 and styled as an 800px / 20% grid at /tmp/conference-website-template/assets/main.css:89-91. Current ATLAS uses four in-page anchor links (`#overview`, `#motivation`, `#scope`, `#difference`) at /Users/yashturkar/Workspace/atlas-corl-2026/index.html:33-48 and widens the table to 900px with 25% cells at /Users/yashturkar/Workspace/atlas-corl-2026/assets/main.css:148-158. This is a real template deviation, but docs/product-specs/workshop-website.md explicitly says the current site is a single-page homepage with in-page anchor navigation, so Builder should preserve anchor behavior and only restore template-like proportions/styling unless the product spec changes.

Content structure now introduces custom hierarchy and callout patterns absent from the upstream template. Upstream homepage is a flat sequence of `h2` + paragraph blocks plus a sponsors table at /tmp/conference-website-template/index.html:47-92. Current ATLAS wraps content in `<main>` and `<section>` blocks, inserts a standalone subtitle line, and highlights selected paragraphs with `.quote` / `.question` callouts at /Users/yashturkar/Workspace/atlas-corl-2026/index.html:50-127, supported by custom rules at /Users/yashturkar/Workspace/atlas-corl-2026/assets/main.css:141-168. These changes make the page feel more editorial than template-like.

The layout system is mostly inherited, but a few tuned dimensions change the visual rhythm: current list width/padding is 860px plus bullet indentation versus the template’s 800px unstyled list baseline (/Users/yashturkar/Workspace/atlas-corl-2026/assets/main.css:70-78 vs /tmp/conference-website-template/assets/main.css:50-53), and current mobile rules expand body/banner/navigation to full width with internal padding (/Users/yashturkar/Workspace/atlas-corl-2026/assets/main.css:176-217) instead of the template’s font-size-only mobile adjustments (/tmp/conference-website-template/assets/main.css:120-128). The responsive rewrite is a deviation, but it is not the main source of template drift.

Current docs support a stronger ATLAS identity while staying recognizably template-based. docs/design-docs/site-architecture.md says to keep the site recognizably based on the upstream template while allowing a stronger visual identity, and docs/product-specs/workshop-website.md says the first release is a static single-page site with in-page anchors. That means Builder should treat multi-page restoration as out of scope unless docs are changed.

follow_up_recommendations: Builder should restore the upstream banner structure in HTML first: replace the custom `.banner-panel` wrapper with the template’s direct banner composition, add back a banner image element, and split the ATLAS masthead into template-like spans so the CSS can mirror the original title treatment while keeping ATLAS text and placeholder logistics.

Builder should revert the banner CSS toward the upstream rules for `.banner`, `.top-left`, `.bottom-right`, `.title1`, `.title2`, and `.year`, then apply only minimal ATLAS-specific color/text substitutions instead of maintaining the custom gradient panel system.

Builder should keep single-page anchor navigation, but restyle it to the template proportions: return `table.navigation` to the upstream 800px rhythm and use the template navigation typography/padding model. Only add or remove nav items if the intended section list changes.

Builder should reduce custom homepage presentation chrome by converting `.subtitle-block`, `.quote`, and `.question` into ordinary template-like text treatment unless there is a strong content reason to keep them. The ATLAS copy itself can remain unchanged.

Builder does not need to restore unused upstream CSS modules such as sponsors, flyer, registration, or full program-table styling yet. The minimum useful alignment work is limited to banner, navigation, and homepage text rhythm.
memory_worthy: Single-page anchor navigation is an intentional product-level deviation from the upstream conference template and should not be reverted without a docs/spec change.
The highest-value template-alignment work is banner/title restoration; navigation and text-rhythm cleanup are secondary.
